examples: More people today carry a watch on their wrists than in their pockets; some people today don't use a watch at all, because their smartphone serves the purpose well enough.
He set the alarm on his watch to 8:00 am.

definitions.text: The act of guarding and observing someone or something.
To look at, see, or view for a period of time.
examples: Watching the clock will not make time go faster.

definitions.text: To observe over a period of time; to notice or pay attention.
examples: Put a little baking soda in some vinegar and watch what happens.
Please watch my suitcase for a minute.
He has to watch the kids that afternoon.
